title: US Stock Market: OpenAI growth concerns spark broad selloff in AI-linked stocks
description: A broad selloff swept across artificial intelligence–linked stocks after a report indicated that OpenAI failed to meet key sales and user growth targets, raising fresh concerns about whether massive investments in the technology will generate meaningful returns in the near term, according to Bloomberg.
content:

title: China Internet Civilization Conference 2026 to Release AI Ethics and Safety Guidelines
description: The 2026 China Internet Civilization Conference in Nanning will release the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Application Ethics and Safety Guidelines (Version 1.0).
content: The 2026 China Internet Civilization Conference is set to take place from May 19 to May 20, 2026. The event will be held in Nanning and will run for about a day and a half. It will bring together experts, officials, and industry leaders to talk about... [1585 chars]

title: Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman head to court in high-stakes showdown over AI
description: OAKLAND, California — Elon Musk, the Tesla CEO, world's richest man and OpenAI cofounder, took the stand Tuesday in a high-stakes trial revolving around a bitter feud with his former friend Sam Altman that could reshape the future development of artificial intelligence.

title: UPDATE 2-White House drafts guidance to bypass Anthropic's risk flag for new AI models, Axios reports
description: The White ​House is developing guidance that could ‌allow ​federal agencies to sidestep Anthropic's supply-chain risk designation and onboard new artificial intelligence models, including Mythos, Axios reported on Tuesday. A draft executive ‌action under consideration could provide the Trump administration with a pathway to de-escalate its dispute with Anthropic, Axios reported, citing two sources familiar with the matter.
content:

title: Sal Khan announces a new AI degree, backed by Big Tech to rival Ivy League — and it costs just $10,000
description: A new AI-focused degree from the Khan TED Institute promises elite-level learning at under $10,000, targeting students priced out of top universities. Backed by major employers, it aims to bridge the widening gap between higher education and the fast-changing job market shaped by artificial intelligence.
content: For decades, the path after school has felt almost pre-written: secure good grades, get into a reputable university, and trust that a degree will open doors. But that certainty is beginning to fracture. Rising tuition costs, shifting job demands, and... [4286 chars]
